description_long: This object is a classic cast iron radiator, featuring a series of eight vertical, rounded columns connected at the top and bottom by horizontal manifolds. The radiator is mounted on a flat rectangular backplate, likely for wall installation, and has two visible valve assemblies on the right side—one at the top and one at the bottom—used for water inlet and outlet control. The columns are evenly spaced and have a ribbed design to maximize surface area for heat dissipation. The finish is a light metallic or painted white, with some darker metallic elements at the base and valve areas. The overall construction appears robust and industrial, suitable for heating rooms in urban buildings. The backplate is smooth and unadorned, serving as a mounting surface rather than a decorative element.
description: A classic wall-mounted cast iron radiator with eight vertical columns, ribbed for heat dissipation, and two visible control valves on a smooth rectangular backplate.
description_view_0: Front view showing all eight vertical columns, the ribbed design, and both control valves on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the radiator's depth, the protruding columns, and the mounting backplate's thickness.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, unadorned backplate with no visible radiator details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the radiator's profile and the alignment of the columns with the backplate.
attribute_pipe_connections: two, right side, top and bottom, horizontal orientation
